examples.sentence: In the corporate meeting, the new project slowly edged out the older initiatives, making them seem less relevant.
examples.translation: During the corporate meeting, the new project gradually marginalized the older initiatives, making them seem less important.

examples.sentence: She felt that her contributions were being edged out by the younger team members who were getting all the attention.
examples.translation: She felt that her contributions were being pushed aside by the younger team members who received all the attention.

examples.sentence: If we’re not careful, automation could edge out many traditional jobs over the next decade.
examples.translation: If we are not careful, automation might gradually marginalize many traditional jobs within the next ten years.

examples.sentence: He carefully edged out of the crowded room to avoid drawing attention.
examples.translation: He moved slowly and cautiously to the edge of the crowded room to avoid drawing attention.

examples.sentence: During the fire drill, the students edged out of the building in an orderly manner.
examples.translation: During the fire drill, the students moved slowly and cautiously to the edge of the building.

examples.sentence: She is edging out onto the narrow balcony to get a better view of the parade below.
examples.translation: She is moving cautiously to the edge of the narrow balcony to see the parade better.

examples.sentence: Will you edge out of the cave carefully? The rocks are unstable near the exit.
examples.translation: Will you move cautiously to the edge of the cave? The rocks are unstable near the exit.

examples.sentence: The lease expired by effluxion of time, so the tenants had to vacate the apartment without any formal notice.
examples.translation: The lease ended simply because the fixed period expired, so the tenants had to leave without formal notice.

examples.sentence: By effluxion of time, the contract terminated last month, and neither party needed to take further action.
examples.translation: The contract ended last month just by the passage of the fixed term, and no further action was required.

examples.sentence: If the agreement is not renewed, it will end automatically by effluxion of time at the end of the year.
examples.translation: If the agreement isn't renewed, it will automatically expire at the end of the year simply because the fixed term ends.

examples.sentence: With the effluxion of time, many traditions have faded away in the village.
examples.translation: Over the passage of time, many traditions have gradually disappeared in the village.

examples.sentence: The historian reflected on how the effluxion of time changes societies beyond recognition.
examples.translation: The historian considered how the lapse of time transforms societies significantly.

examples.sentence: Effluxion of time has shown that patience often leads to better outcomes.
examples.translation: The passage of time has demonstrated that patience usually results in improved outcomes.

examples.sentence: The fixed-term employment contract ended by effluxion of time, so the company did not renew it.
examples.translation: The employment contract expired at the end of the fixed period simply due to the passage of time, so it was not renewed.

examples.sentence: By effluxion of time, the patent rights ceased without any additional legal action required.
examples.translation: The patent rights expired at the end of the term by the mere passage of time, without further legal steps.

examples.sentence: The lease agreement was terminated automatically by effluxion of time last June.
examples.translation: The lease agreement ended automatically last June because the fixed term expired.

examples.sentence: Unless extended, the license will expire through effluxion of time on the specified date.
examples.translation: Unless renewed, the license will end on the specified date simply due to the passage of the fixed period.

examples.sentence: The kids egged him on to climb the tall tree despite his fear of heights.
examples.translation: The kids encouraged him to climb the tall tree even though he was afraid of heights.

examples.sentence: Don’t egg me on to take unnecessary risks when I’m already nervous about the outcome.
examples.translation: Please don’t encourage me to take foolish risks when I’m already anxious about what might happen.

examples.sentence: She was egged on by her friends to audition for the play, even though she wasn’t confident.
examples.translation: Her friends encouraged her to try out for the play, despite her lack of confidence.

examples.sentence: He carefully egged the freshly laid eggs onto the tray to avoid breaking them.
examples.translation: He gently placed the freshly laid eggs onto the tray to keep them from breaking.

examples.sentence: The chef egged the omelette onto the plate with a smooth motion.
examples.translation: The chef transferred the omelette onto the plate smoothly.

examples.sentence: Can you egg the basket with these eggs before we leave for the picnic?
examples.translation: Could you put these eggs into the basket before we go on the picnic?

examples.sentence: She egged several eggs into the mixing bowl to prepare the batter.
examples.translation: She cracked and added several eggs into the mixing bowl to make the batter.

examples.sentence: His plan to become a millionaire overnight was just eggs in moonshine—completely unrealistic.
examples.translation: His idea to get rich quickly was just a fanciful and unrealistic notion.

examples.sentence: When they talked about colonizing Mars next year, I thought it sounded like eggs in moonshine.
examples.translation: Their discussion about colonizing Mars soon seemed like an absurd and fanciful idea.

examples.sentence: Emma dismissed his wild theories as nothing more than eggs in moonshine.
examples.translation: Emma rejected his outrageous theories as fanciful and unrealistic.

examples.sentence: The distillery specialized in making traditional eggs in moonshine, a unique local delicacy.
examples.translation: The distillery produced a special local dish involving eggs mixed with moonshine.

examples.sentence: She carefully placed the eggs in moonshine to infuse them with flavor overnight.
examples.translation: She put the eggs into moonshine to soak and absorb the flavor during the night.

examples.sentence: The recipe calls for eggs in moonshine, which gives the dish a distinct aroma and taste.
examples.translation: The recipe requires combining eggs with moonshine to create a unique flavor.

examples.sentence: At the festival, they served eggs in moonshine as a traditional treat from the region.
examples.translation: During the festival, eggs mixed with moonshine were served as a customary local delicacy.

examples.sentence: It was either do or die for the team in the final minutes of the championship game.
examples.translation: The team had to act with complete urgency and determination at the last minutes of the championship game.

examples.sentence: If we want to meet the deadline, it’s either do or die starting now.
examples.translation: To meet the deadline, we must proceed with extreme urgency and determination immediately.

examples.sentence: She approached her job interview with an either do or die attitude, knowing it could change her life.
examples.translation: She faced her interview with total determination, aware the outcome would be decisive.

examples.sentence: The captain shouted ‘Either do or die!’ as he ordered the troops to charge the enemy line.
examples.translation: The captain gave a direct ultimatum: carry out the attack or face death.

examples.sentence: The rebels were told, ‘Either do or die,’ emphasizing the fatal consequences of failure.
examples.translation: The rebels received a strict order to act or be killed.

examples.sentence: In that desperate moment, the leader’s command was clear: either do or die without hesitation.
examples.translation: The leader’s instruction was an explicit ultimatum to act decisively or face death.

examples.sentence: Will you either do or die when faced with such an ultimatum, or try to negotiate?
examples.translation: Will you carry out the order or be killed, or attempt to find a compromise?

examples.sentence: He elbowed his way through the crowded subway car to reach the exit.
examples.translation: He pushed and jostled with his elbows to move through the packed subway car to the exit.

examples.sentence: During the busy festival, I was accidentally elbowed in the ribs several times.
examples.translation: At the crowded festival, I was struck in the ribs multiple times by elbows.

examples.sentence: She elbowed him gently to get his attention during the meeting.
examples.translation: She lightly struck him with her elbow to catch his attention in the meeting.

examples.sentence: The boxer elbowed his opponent in the heat of the fight, trying to gain an advantage.
examples.translation: The boxer struck his opponent with his elbow during the intense fight to gain advantage.

examples.sentence: At the concert, he elbowed his way to the front to get a better view of the stage.
examples.translation: He forced his way through the crowd using his elbows to reach the front for a better view.

examples.sentence: She elbowed past the queue, making room for herself among the impatient shoppers.
examples.translation: She nudged others aside with her elbows to create space in the line.

examples.sentence: They elbowed through the dense crowd to catch the last train on time.
examples.translation: They pushed their way through the packed crowd using elbows to reach the train quickly.

examples.sentence: I was elbowed out of the way when the doors opened and everyone rushed inside.
examples.translation: I was pushed aside by elbows as the doors opened and people hurried in.

examples.sentence: The new manager elbowed his way into the leadership team, pushing out some of the old guard.
examples.translation: The new manager aggressively displaced some senior members to gain a leadership position.

examples.sentence: She felt elbowed out of the decision-making process by her more assertive colleagues.
examples.translation: She felt pushed aside and excluded by her more forceful coworkers in decisions.

examples.sentence: When he elbowed other candidates aside, it was clear he was determined to secure the promotion.
examples.translation: By aggressively pushing others aside, he showed strong ambition to get the promotion.

examples.sentence: Cleaning the old windows took a lot of elbow grease, but they finally shone like new.
examples.translation: The windows required strenuous physical effort to clean, but they ended up shining.

examples.sentence: You’ll need some serious elbow grease to get that stain out of the carpet.
examples.translation: You will have to work very hard, scrubbing vigorously, to remove that stain.

examples.sentence: They put in plenty of elbow grease to restore the vintage car to its original condition.
examples.translation: They applied strenuous manual labor to refurbish the old car completely.

examples.sentence: He applied some elbow grease ointment to soothe the aching joint after the workout.
examples.translation: He rubbed a special grease or ointment onto his elbow area to relieve pain after exercise.

examples.sentence: The mechanic used elbow grease lubricant on the rusty hinge to make it move smoothly again.
examples.translation: The mechanic put grease on the elbow joint of the machinery to ease its movement.

examples.sentence: After washing, she carefully wiped the elbow grease off her skin to avoid irritation.
examples.translation: After applying grease to her elbow, she cleaned it off to prevent skin irritation.

examples.sentence: The therapist recommended using elbow grease balm to reduce inflammation in the elbow region.
examples.translation: The therapist advised applying a special balm to the elbow to lessen swelling.

examples.sentence: His elbow mentality made it difficult for him to work cooperatively with the rest of the team.
examples.translation: His self-serving attitude caused problems in collaborating with his colleagues.

examples.sentence: The company’s culture of elbow mentality encouraged employees to prioritize personal gain over teamwork.
examples.translation: The organization’s focus on self-interest led workers to put their own advantage before group welfare.

examples.sentence: Without addressing the elbow mentality among executives, the firm struggled to foster a collaborative environment.
examples.translation: Unless the selfish mindset of leaders was changed, the company couldn’t create teamwork.

examples.sentence: The rise of the electronic superhighway has transformed how we access information globally.
examples.translation: The development of high-capacity digital networks has changed worldwide information access.

examples.sentence: With the electronic superhighway expanding, businesses can communicate instantly across continents.
examples.translation: As digital communication infrastructure grows, companies can exchange information instantly worldwide.

examples.sentence: Many educators promote digital literacy to help students navigate the electronic superhighway effectively.
examples.translation: Teachers encourage students to learn skills to use the internet and related networks efficiently.

examples.sentence: The electronic superhighway is often depicted as a vast network of cables and satellites transmitting data at incredible speeds.
examples.translation: The digital network is conceptualized as an extensive infrastructure rapidly conveying information.

examples.sentence: In his lecture, Dr. Chen compared the internet to an electronic superhighway linking cities and countries worldwide.
examples.translation: Dr. Chen described the internet metaphorically as a high-speed network connecting global locations.

examples.sentence: The rapid expansion of the electronic superhighway has led to new opportunities in e-commerce and telemedicine.
examples.translation: Growth of digital communication systems has created new fields in online business and remote healthcare.

examples.sentence: Understanding the electronic superhighway helps us appreciate the complexity behind modern telecommunications.
examples.translation: Knowing about digital networks helps us grasp how advanced modern communication systems are.

examples.sentence: The sudden thunderstorm was an element of surprise that caught all the hikers off guard.
examples.translation: The unexpected thunderstorm was a factor that astonished the hikers.

examples.sentence: Her unexpected arrival added an element of surprise to the birthday party.
examples.translation: Her coming without warning was a component that amazed the guests at the party.

examples.sentence: The magician’s trick included an element of surprise that thrilled the audience.
examples.translation: The magician’s act contained a factor producing astonishment and excitement.

examples.sentence: In the recipe, adding chili powder introduces an element of surprise to the mild dish.
examples.translation: Including chili powder adds a surprising flavor component to the otherwise mild food.

examples.sentence: The team used the element of surprise to launch a successful ambush on their opponents.
examples.translation: The team gained a strategic advantage by catching their opponents unprepared.

examples.sentence: By attacking at dawn, the soldiers exploited the element of surprise effectively.
examples.translation: The soldiers used the unpreparedness of the enemy at dawn as a strategic advantage.

examples.sentence: The negotiator’s sudden change in demands introduced an element of surprise that shifted the talks.
examples.translation: The unexpected alteration by the negotiator gave them a strategic edge in negotiations.

examples.sentence: The circus amazed the children with a live elephant in the room performing tricks.
examples.translation: The circus entertained children with an actual elephant physically present inside the venue.

examples.sentence: We had to rearrange the furniture to make space for the elephant in the room during the photo shoot.
examples.translation: We moved the furniture to accommodate the live elephant physically inside the room for photos.

examples.sentence: The trainer carefully guided the elephant in the room through the narrow doorway.
examples.translation: The trainer led the live elephant physically present inside the room out through the small door.

examples.sentence: At the zoo exhibit, visitors were thrilled to see a real elephant in the room up close.
examples.translation: At the zoo, people were excited to see a live elephant physically within the enclosure.

examples.sentence: During the tense meeting, the budget cuts were the elephant in the room nobody dared to mention.
examples.translation: At the meeting, the obvious problem of budget cuts was deliberately ignored by everyone.

examples.sentence: We all knew about the company’s failing sales, but the elephant in the room was never discussed openly.
examples.translation: Everyone was aware of poor sales, yet the significant issue was avoided in conversation.

examples.sentence: Addressing the elephant in the room, the CEO finally spoke about the layoffs everyone feared.
examples.translation: The CEO confronted the obvious and uncomfortable topic of layoffs that everyone had been avoiding.

examples.sentence: They submitted the project report at the eleventh hour, just minutes before the deadline closed.
examples.translation: They turned in the report at the very last possible moment before the deadline.

examples.sentence: The agreement was signed at the eleventh hour, avoiding a costly legal battle.
examples.translation: The contract was finalized at the last moment, preventing a lawsuit.

examples.sentence: If you wait until the eleventh hour to prepare, you might not do your best work.
examples.translation: Delaying preparation until the last possible moment could harm the quality of your work.

examples.sentence: The clock struck the eleventh hour, and the meeting was set to begin promptly.
examples.translation: The time reached eleven o'clock, marking the scheduled start of the meeting.

examples.sentence: She glanced at the watch and realized it was already the eleventh hour.
examples.translation: She saw that the time was eleven o'clock.

examples.sentence: By the eleventh hour, the streets were quiet as most shops had closed for the night.
examples.translation: At eleven o'clock, the streets became quiet because shops had shut.

examples.sentence: The train departs exactly at the eleventh hour, so don’t be late.
examples.translation: The train leaves at eleven o'clock sharp, so punctuality is important.

examples.sentence: The eleventh-hour deal saved the company from bankruptcy at the last possible moment.
examples.translation: The deal arranged just before the deadline prevented the company from failing.

examples.sentence: They made an eleventh-hour decision to postpone the event due to bad weather.
examples.translation: They decided at the last moment to delay the event because of the weather.

examples.sentence: An eleventh-hour agreement was reached after weeks of tough negotiations.
examples.translation: A last-minute agreement was finalized after prolonged discussions.

examples.sentence: The clock showed the eleventh-hour as everyone prepared for the ceremony to begin.
examples.translation: The time was eleven o'clock while everyone got ready for the ceremony.

examples.sentence: She always drinks her afternoon tea at the eleventh hour without fail.
examples.translation: She habitually drinks tea exactly at eleven o'clock in the afternoon.

examples.sentence: The eleventh-hour bell rang signaling the start of the class.
examples.translation: The bell at eleven o'clock announced the beginning of the lesson.

examples.sentence: At the eleventh hour, the town clock chimed loudly across the square.
examples.translation: The clock in the town square rang loudly at eleven o'clock.

examples.sentence: The announcer declared, ‘Elvis has left the building,’ signaling the end of the concert.
examples.translation: The announcer stated that Elvis Presley had exited the venue, marking the concert’s conclusion.

examples.sentence: After the final encore, the phrase ‘Elvis has left the building’ was broadcast to inform fans the show was over.
examples.translation: Following the last performance, the message was given that Elvis was no longer present and the event had ended.

examples.sentence: When the band finished playing, someone jokingly said, ‘Elvis has left the building,’ to indicate no more music was coming.
examples.translation: After the band stopped, a person humorously remarked that the performance was finished.

examples.sentence: The security guard announced, ‘Elvis has left the building,’ as the crowd began to disperse.
examples.translation: The guard announced that Elvis was gone, prompting the audience to leave.

examples.sentence: After the final speaker, the moderator said, ‘Elvis has left the building,’ to signal the event was over.
examples.translation: The moderator used the phrase to indicate that the event had concluded.

examples.sentence: Once the last customer left, the shopkeeper sighed and muttered, ‘Elvis has left the building.’
examples.translation: When the last customer departed, the shopkeeper expressed that business was finished.

examples.sentence: The phrase ‘Elvis has left the building’ has become a way to say the party is definitely over.
examples.translation: This phrase is commonly used to indicate that an event or activity has ended.

examples.sentence: According to myth, the souls of the righteous rested peacefully in the Elysian Fields after death.
examples.translation: In mythology, virtuous souls dwelled happily in the Elysian Fields after passing away.

examples.sentence: The poet described the Elysian Fields as a paradise where heroes enjoyed eternal bliss.
examples.translation: The poet depicted the Elysian Fields as a heavenly place for heroes to live forever happily.

examples.sentence: In the ancient texts, the Elysian Fields are portrayed as a beautiful and serene afterlife realm.
examples.translation: Ancient writings show the Elysian Fields as a calm and lovely place for the blessed after death.

examples.sentence: The artist’s painting depicted the Elysian Fields with lush greenery and peaceful skies.
examples.translation: The artwork represented the Elysian Fields as a green, tranquil paradise.
